title: GenAI Inference API (Generic)
description: Integrate with a third-party GenAI inference API (LLM, image/video generation, embeddings) rather than hosting models directly. Abstracts provider choice behind a common interface; select a provider variant for latency, cost, and capability trade-offs.

cost.costNotes: Choose provider based on model availability, pricing, latency requirements, and free tier needs. Implement usage monitoring and caching to control costs.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Min $0 represents free tier usage (e.g., HuggingFace Inference API free tier, Replicate small quotas). Low usage scenarios ($10-100/mo) cover ~100-1000 API calls for text generation or ~50-500 image generations. Medium usage ($100-1000/mo) represents production applications with moderate traffic: ~10K-100K text API calls or ~1K-10K image generations. Max $5000 represents high-volume production usage: ~500K+ LLM API calls, ~50K+ image generations, or intensive fine-tuning workloads. Actual costs highly dependent on: model complexity (GPT-4 vs GPT-3.5), output length, image resolution, and provider pricing (OpenAI $0.01-0.12/1K tokens, Replicate $0.0002-0.02/second, Fal $0.001-0.10/generation).
provenance.adoptionCost: Medium-low complexity integration. Most GenAI APIs provide REST endpoints with good SDKs (Python, Node.js, etc.), making basic integration straightforward (2-5 days). However, production-ready implementation requires: error handling and retry logic (1-2 days), rate limiting and queuing mechanisms (1-2 days), prompt engineering and optimization (2-3 days), cost monitoring and usage tracking (1-2 days), response caching strategies (1-2 days), and multi-provider fallback logic if needed (2-3 days). Total effort: 8-15 developer days at ~$100-150/hour = $6,400-18,000 internal cost, but with AI coding assistants and good documentation, effective implementation time reduces to 5-8 days ($800 represents accelerated adoption cost with modern tooling).
provenance.licenseCost: No upfront license costs for GenAI inference APIs. Major providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Replicate, Fal, HuggingFace, Vertex AI) operate on pay-as-you-go or usage-tied cloud billing models without standalone software license fees. Free tiers or credits may be available from some vendors, while enterprise agreements can introduce spend commitments, but these are consumption commitments rather than license purchases. Open-source self-hosted alternatives (vLLM, TGI) are also $0 license cost but shift costs to infrastructure.

provides.reasoning: This is the core capability of the pattern - providing access to generative AI inference services. The term is specific and distinguishes from training or fine-tuning capabilities.
A specific type of generative AI capability that this pattern explicitly supports. Appropriate for patterns that handle text-to-image or image-to-image generation.
Another specific generative AI capability for LLM inference and text generation. This is a common use case for GenAI inference APIs and semantically appropriate for this pattern.
Many GenAI providers offer audio generation capabilities (text-to-speech, music generation), making this a relevant modality for the generic inference pattern.
Modern GenAI APIs increasingly support multimodal inputs and outputs (e.g., vision+language models), representing an important capability category for this pattern.

requires.reasoning: GenAI inference APIs are typically RESTful HTTP endpoints, so an HTTP client is needed to make requests to these services.
Essential for securely storing and accessing API keys, authentication tokens, and credentials required to authenticate with GenAI provider APIs. This is a critical security requirement.
GenAI APIs typically have strict rate limits and quota management requirements. Implementing rate limiting is essential to avoid service disruptions and manage costs effectively.
Most GenAI providers use API key-based authentication as their primary auth mechanism. This capability is required to properly authenticate requests to these services.
GenAI inference can have variable latency, occasional timeouts, and transient failures. Retry logic with exponential backoff is essential for building resilient integrations.

availability.reason: Generic pattern for public SaaS providers.

model_provider.description: AI model provider - OpenAI (GPT-4, DALL-E), Anthropic (Claude), HuggingFace (open models), Replicate (community models), Fal (fast inference), Vertex AI (Gemini, Imagen, embeddings on GCP)

model_type.description: Type of generative AI task - text-generation (LLMs), image-generation (DALL-E, Stable Diffusion), text-to-image, embeddings (vector representations), multimodal (vision+text)

temperature.description: Sampling temperature controlling randomness - 0 for deterministic/factual outputs, 0.7 for balanced creativity, 1.5-2 for highly creative/diverse outputs

max_tokens.description: Maximum tokens in model response - affects cost and response length. Typical: 512 (short), 2048 (medium), 8192+ (long-form content)

streaming.description: Enable streaming responses for real-time token-by-token output (better UX for chat) vs waiting for complete response (simpler implementation)

service_auth_method.description: Service credential method used to authenticate to the LLM provider API. Always api_key — stored in environment variable, never in code.

supports_nfr.reason: Generic LLM inference pattern cannot guarantee HIPAA compliance without a specific provider BAA; use a HIPAA-eligible provider explicitly.

requires_nfr.reason: GenAI inference APIs are third-party SaaS services typically offering up to 99.9% availability SLAs.
GenAI inference involves complex model computations requiring at least 500ms p95 latency.
P99 latencies for GenAI inference include model queue time and cold starts requiring at least 1000ms.

Multi-region GenAI API patterns with automatic failover achieve RTO of at least 1 minute. Although vendors like Fal Serverless platforms provide immediate automatic recovery through horizontal scaling and job redistribution.

Enterprise GenAI API tiers typically support up to 10K requests/second (36M jobs/hour) with rate limiting. Although vendors like Fal Serverless GenAI can elastically scale to handle up to 100M jobs/hour.

supports_constraints.reason: Pattern specifically provides GenAI inference capabilities and requires this feature flag to be enabled.

requires_constraints.reason: GenAI inference APIs are cloud-agnostic SaaS services accessible from any cloud provider or on-premises environment.
GenAI inference APIs are language-agnostic services accessed via HTTP REST APIs from any programming language.
GenAI inference APIs are platform-agnostic cloud services callable from any platform type.
